---
title: "Investment Insights Dashboard"
author: Elvis Safary
format:
    dashboard:
        embed-resources: true
        orientation: rows
theme: Sandstone
execute:
    echo: false
    warning: false
---

<style>
.value-box-value { font-size: 1.2rem !important; }
.value-box-title { font-size: 0.8rem !important; }
</style>

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import itables.options as opt
from itables import show

#load data
investment_data = pd.read_csv('Finance_data.csv')

#data cleaning
investment_data.columns = investment_data.columns.str.strip()

#KPI calculations
total_respondents = len(investment_data)
stock_market_pct = (investment_data['Stock_Marktet'] == 'Yes').mean() * 100
top_avenue = investment_data['Avenue'].mode()[0]
most_common_expect = investment_data['Expect'].mode()[0]

top_objective = investment_data['Objective'].mode()[0]
top_duration = investment_data['Duration'].mode()[0]
top_factor = investment_data['Factor'].mode()[0]
top_source = investment_data['Source'].mode()[0]

#data preparation for visuals

#top 5 Investment Options (by Avg Ranking - lower is better rank)
ranking_cols = ['Mutual_Funds', 'Equity_Market', 'Debentures', 'Government_Bonds', 'Fixed_Deposits', 'PPF', 'Gold']
avg_ranks = investment_data[ranking_cols].mean().sort_values().head(5).reset_index()
avg_ranks.columns = ['Avenue', 'Avg_Rank']

#replace underscores for display
avg_ranks['Avenue'] = avg_ranks['Avenue'].str.replace('_', ' ')

#age Grouping for duration analysis
def age_group(age):
    if age <= 25: return '20-25'
    elif age <= 30: return '26-30'
    else: return '31-35'

investment_data['age_group'] = investment_data['age'].apply(age_group)

#visualizations
#gender distribution pie chart
fig_gender = px.pie(investment_data, names='gender', title="Gender Distribution",           color='gender', color_discrete_map={'Male': '#1e3a8a', 'Female': '#10b981'}, hole=0.4)
fig_gender.update_traces(textposition='inside', textinfo='percent+label')
fig_gender.update_layout(showlegend=False)

# top 5 preferred options-bar chart
fig_top_5 = px.bar(avg_ranks, x='Avg_Rank', y='Avenue', orientation='h', title="Top 5 Preferred Investment Options", labels={'Avg_Rank': 'Average Rank (Lower is Better)', 'Avenue': 'Avenue'}, color='Avg_Rank', color_continuous_scale='GnBu_r')
fig_top_5.update_layout(showlegend=False, template="plotly_white", yaxis={'categoryorder':'total descending'})

#investment objective by gender-stacked bar chart
fig_objective = px.histogram(investment_data, x="Objective", color="gender", barmode="stack", title="Investment Objective by Gender", color_discrete_map={'Male': '#1e3a8a', 'Female': '#10b981'})
fig_objective.update_layout(template="plotly_white", xaxis_title="Objective", yaxis_title="Count")

#investment duration by age group-column chart
fig_duration = px.histogram(investment_data, x="age_group", color="Duration", barmode="group", title="Investment Duration by Age Group", category_orders={"age_group": ["20-25", "26-30", "31-35"]}, color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Prism)
fig_duration.update_layout(template="plotly_white", xaxis_title="Age Group", yaxis_title="Number of Respondents")

#expected return percentages-donut chart
fig_expect = px.pie(investment_data, names='Expect', title="Expected Return Percentages", hole=0.5, color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Safe)
fig_expect.update_traces(textposition='outside', textinfo='percent+label', pull=[0.05, 0, 0, 0])
fig_expect.update_layout(showlegend=True, legend_title_text='Return Range',
    margin=dict(t=50, b=20, l=20, r=20))
```

# Investor overview
## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Respondents"
dict(icon="people", color="info", value=total_respondents)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Stock Market Investors"
dict(icon="graph-up-arrow", color="#10b981", value=f"{stock_market_pct:.1f}%")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Top Avenue"
dict(icon="trophy", color="info", value=top_avenue)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Most Frequent Expectation"
dict(icon="bullseye", color="#ef4444", value=most_common_expect)
```

## Row {height="80%"}
### {width="60%"}

```{python}
fig_top_5
```

### {width="40%"}

```{python}
fig_gender
```

# Strategy and Trend
## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Primary Objective"
dict(icon="bullseye", color="primary", value=top_objective)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Preferred Duration"
dict(icon="clock-history", color="#fde047", value=top_duration)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Influencing Factor"
dict(icon="shield-check", color="success", value=top_factor)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Main Information Source"
dict(icon="broadcast", color="#bfdbfe", value=top_source)
```

## Row {height="40%"}

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_objective
```

### {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_expect
```

## Row {height="40%"}
### {width="100%"}
```{python}
fig_duration
```

# Data Source

```{python}
opt.features = ["paging", "ordering", "info", "buttons"]

show(investment_data[['gender', 'age', 'Investment_Avenues', 'Stock_Marktet', 'Objective', 'Duration', 'Expect', 'Avenue']], classes="display nowrap compact", buttons=["csv", "excel"])
```
